sources.label: KSB press releases (no new releases since Jun 6)
Impeller.net - KSB AGM: record 2025, subdued start to 2026 (pre-Jun 6 context)
aktien.guide - KSB Vz analyst targets ~EUR 1,127 avg vs ~EUR 860-878 quote; ATH EUR 1,245 on Mar 17, 2026

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since Jun 6 and yesterday's down move (adjusted-score reconciliation, rank 11 -> 16) is now applied; today's -2.2% drift on no news plus a ~30% de-rate from the March high modestly improves entry asymmetry on an intact liquid-cooling/water-reuse pump thesis, warranting a small positive delta but no re-rank.
verdicts.bull: Under-owned EUR 1.7B pumps/valves maker with Q1 2026 order intake up 15.2% and management explicitly flagging data-center cooling and semiconductor water infrastructure as structural growth; analyst average target ~EUR 1,127 implies ~28-31% upside from EUR 878.
verdicts.neutral: Data-center cooling remains a small slice of revenue today; the stock drifts with European industrial sentiment, and the strict-screen adjusted score already discounts the displayed rank, so no fresh evidence forces a move either way.
verdicts.bear: AGM commentary flagged a subdued start to 2026 outside the one-off energy order; if liquid-cooling component demand consolidates around specialist incumbents (e.g., dedicated CDU/coolant players), KSB's general industrial pump mix never converts the AI optionality and the -9.5% YTD keeps grinding.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: H1 2026 results (mid-August) showing data-center/semiconductor water-infrastructure orders becoming a named, quantified bucket, or a disclosed hyperscale cooling-loop contract win.
verdicts.primaryRisk: AI exposure stays immaterial to the P&L while core European industrial/chemical end-markets soften, leaving it a cyclical pump maker mislabeled as an AI bottleneck node.
